The shift gives brands and marketers a cleaner identity layer for WhatsApp Business outreach, reducing the friction of phone-number capture that has limited opt-in conversation flows on the channel.

title: Gemini can now take notes in Google Meet for Google AI Pro and Ultra subscribers.
blurb: Google Meet now offers "Take notes for me" to Google AI Pro and Ultra subscribers, with Gemini transcribing calls in real time, generating a summary with action items, and saving it to a Google Doc plus an emailed recap. Meeting hosts enable it per-call via the pencil icon or default-on in settings, and all participants get notified when note-taking is active.

title: YouTube Shorts Dislike Data Stops Updating by Month’s End as Platform Overhauls Viewer Feedback Layer
blurb: YouTube will stop updating Shorts dislike counts in YouTube Studio by the end of June, pointing creators instead toward Likes and a views-to-swipe-away ratio as the primary performance signals. The platform is also adding reason-specific input to the "Not Interested" option to feed its recommendation engine, so swipe-away rate, not dislikes, now drives short-form distribution.

title: WhatsApp Opens Username Reservations to 3 Billion Users
blurb: WhatsApp began letting its 3 billion users reserve usernames, allowing people to connect without exposing their phone numbers. The shift moves WhatsApp toward the identity model already standard on Telegram, Instagram, and Signal, and gives product teams building on the platform a stable handle to key contacts against rather than a mutable phone number.

title: YouTube Shorts Dislike Data Stops Updating by Month’s End as Platform Overhauls Viewer Feedback Layer
blurb: YouTube will stop updating Shorts dislike counts in YouTube Studio by the end of June, pointing creators instead toward Likes and a views-to-swipe-aways ratio as the primary performance signals. The change adds a sub-layer to the viewer "Not Interested" option, where specific dismissal reasons now feed the recommendation engine, so PMs tracking Shorts performance will need to rebuild dashboards around engagement metrics rather than dislikes.

title: WhatsApp introduces usernames so you can chat without phone numbers
blurb: WhatsApp is adding usernames so people can message contacts without exchanging phone numbers, opening reservations this week ahead of a full rollout in the coming months. The move follows Signal's 2024 launch of the same feature and adds an optional username key, giving product teams building on WhatsApp a more privacy-friendly identity layer for business and creator accounts across its three billion users.

title: Europe’s unlicensed crypto firms face ‘wipeout’ as MiCA deadline hits
blurb: ESMA called on unauthorized crypto-asset service providers to wind down operations in an orderly manner as MiCA's transitional period ends July 1, stripping pre-MiCA national registrations across the EEA. Of more than 3,000 registered VASPs in 2024, only 244 firms now hold MiCA licenses, and OKX Europe's CEO estimates 80% of players won't survive the combined licensing and compliance burden.

title: OKX and Coinbase Race to Sign Up Binance's Displaced EU Users Before MiCA Deadline
blurb: OKX and Coinbase launched competing deposit-bonus campaigns to capture EU users losing access to Binance, which will restrict services across the bloc starting July 1 after withdrawing its MiCA license application in Greece. OKX is offering tiered USDC rewards up to 8% on deposits to $500,000, while Coinbase is targeting Coinbase One subscribers in seven countries, both running through July 13.

title: BNY Adds USDC to Institutional Custody Platform in Expanded Circle Partnership
blurb: BNY, the largest custodian bank with $59.4T under custody, made USDC the first stablecoin on its Digital Asset Custody platform, letting institutional clients store, transfer, mint, and redeem Circle's token alongside traditional assets in one environment. The move follows BNY's January tokenized-deposit launch and the GENIUS Act, which lets federally chartered banks custody payment stablecoins, with BNY planning to add more issuers over time.

title: India’s USDT premium tops 8.5% as crypto remittance crackdown squeezes stablecoin supply: report
blurb: India's USDT premium climbed above 8.5%, more than double its typical 3-4% range, after Enforcement Directorate raids on Bengaluru crypto remittance firms disrupted the domestic stablecoin supply pipeline. The ED alleges the firms moved over $265M in unauthorized cross-border transfers using USDT to bypass FEMA channels, with market makers pulling back on overseas USDT purchases ahead of a July 2 parliamentary review of virtual digital asset rules.

title: BNY and Circle expand partnership, adding mint and burn capabilities for USDC
blurb: BNY Mellon expanded its partnership with Circle to let institutional clients mint and burn USDC directly through the bank's Digital Asset Custody platform, with USDC becoming the first supported asset. Clients can now custody USDC in BNY wallets and instruct the world's largest custodian to convert dollars into newly minted USDC or redeem it back, with additional stablecoin issuers planned over time.

title: BlackRock Adds Ethena's Synthetic Dollar to Its $20T Aladdin Risk Management Platform
blurb: BlackRock added Ethena's USDe synthetic dollar as an approved digital asset on Aladdin, its institutional risk platform spanning more than $20T in assets, opening the $4.45B-supply token to the banks, insurers, and pension funds that run money on the system. The deal also makes BlackRock's tokenized Treasury fund BUIDL the primary backing for Ethena's whitelabel stablecoins, with Ethena committing a $100M liquidity facility for BlackRock's tokenized products.

title: BlackRock’s Aladdin platform adds deeper support for Ethena’s stablecoin products
blurb: BlackRock added support for Ethena's USDe synthetic dollar to its Aladdin platform and set up a $100M liquidity facility through Securitize that lets eligible BUIDL clients swap BUIDL tokens for USDC, USDtb, and other stablecoins outside normal market hours. The arrangement deepens BlackRock's tokenized-Treasury push, where BUIDL already anchors a tokenized Treasury market worth roughly $15B.

title: 236,000 DCloud Uni-App Sites Used in Crypto Scams, Phishing, and Wallet Drainers
blurb: Infoblox identified 236,493 distinct domains running investment-scam templates built on DCloud Uni-App, a legitimate Chinese cross-platform framework, powering fake crypto exchanges, pig-butchering operations, phishing networks, and wallet drainers since mid-2022. The set includes RainbowEx, the bogus exchange behind a 2024 Ponzi scheme that hit tens of thousands in San Pedro, Argentina, and led to seven arrests.

title: MiCA's looming deadline could leave 10 million crypto users without a platform in the EU
blurb: MiCA takes full effect across the EU on July 1, forcing dozens of unlicensed exchanges to halt or restrict services and potentially displacing more than 10 million users, with executives estimating up to 80% of Europe's roughly 3,000 pre-MiCA VASPs won't survive the deadline. The European Banking Authority separately proposed fines of up to 12.5% of annual turnover for major stablecoin issuers that breach the rules.

title: Strategy Authorizes up to $1.25B of Bitcoin Sales as Saylor Formalizes Capital Pivot
blurb: Strategy's board approved a "Digital Credit Capital Framework" authorizing up to $1.25B in Bitcoin sales to fund dividends, interest, and buybacks, formalizing that the largest corporate BTC holder may now sell from its 847,363 BTC position. The pivot reverses Saylor's accumulate-and-hold identity as the stack sits roughly $14B underwater and STRC preferred stock trades well below its $100 target, straining the share-sale funding model.

title: BlackRock pushes deeper into DeFi with Ethena integration, sending ENA up 8%
blurb: BlackRock will integrate Ethena's yield-bearing synthetic dollar USDe into its Aladdin risk platform and stand up a $100M liquidity facility letting holders of its tokenized Treasury fund BUIDL swap into USDC, USDtb, and other stablecoins outside market hours. ENA rose 8% on the news, which follows Ethena's recent deals with Coinbase, Janus Henderson, and Securitize.

title: Tether's USDT jumps to 8.5% premium in India after crypto payment crackdown
blurb: India's Enforcement Directorate raided five crypto payment firms in Bengaluru on June 17, alleging $265M in unauthorized cross-border transfers via USDT, which choked the stablecoin's supply into the country. USDT now trades around 102.88 rupees against a 94.65 official dollar rate, an 8.5% premium roughly double the usual 3-4% gap as market makers pull back from sourcing tokens abroad.

title: JPMorgan backs U.S. crypto bill but warns of risks in digital asset framework
blurb: JPMorgan endorsed the Senate's Digital Asset Market Clarity Act but warned that any framework must extend traditional finance safeguards to crypto rather than open new gaps. In a blog post from its payments and digital-assets leads, the bank pushed for bank-like capital, liquidity, and consumer-protection rules on stablecoins and tokenized deposits, plus preserved anti-money-laundering tools as negotiators race to advance the bill before the August recess.

title: Strategy pauses bitcoin buys, establishes $1B digital credit repurchase program as USD reserve tops $2.5B
blurb: Strategy paused its bitcoin purchases between June 22 and June 28 despite raising $1.15B from MSTR stock sales, keeping its holdings flat at 847,363 BTC while lifting its USD reserve to $2.55B. The company also approved a $1B Digital Credit Securities Repurchase Program targeting its STRC, STRF, STRD, and STRK preferred shares, prioritizing STRC, as analysts pressed it to halt BTC buying amid rising dividend obligations.

title: BIS warns stablecoins are more like ETFs than actual money, and they're creating FX risk
blurb: The Bank for International Settlements argued in its annual report that stablecoins function more like ETF shares than money, citing prices that deviate from par and redemptions that aren't guaranteed instant or at face value. The report also warned that dollar-pegged stablecoins are accelerating dollarization in vulnerable economies, undermining local currencies and routing around traditional capital controls.

title: J.P. Morgan broadens blockchain settlement network as banks modernize cross-border payments
blurb: J.P. Morgan added five Asia-Pacific currencies to its Kinexys blockchain settlement platform, bringing total support to eight and letting institutional clients settle payments and FX 24/7 via tokenized bank deposits. The platform, which has processed over $4 trillion in transactions to date, gives multinationals a continuous cross-border rail that bypasses the multi-bank delays and local banking hours of traditional settlement.

title: White House to speak with law enforcement groups to push Crypto's Clarity Act
blurb: The White House will meet with law enforcement groups on Monday to resolve objections to the Senate's Digital Asset Market Clarity Act, focusing on Section 604's protections that shield non-controlling software developers from "money transmitter" liability. Groups like the National Sheriffs Association argue the language hands DeFi, mixers, and tumblers a blanket exemption, a sticking point that has stalled the market structure bill's path through the Senate.

title: Wall Street's BNY expands stablecoin services for institutions, starting with Circle's USDC
blurb: BNY, the world's largest custody bank with $59T in assets under custody, will let institutional clients hold, mint, and redeem Circle's USDC through its Digital Asset Custody platform, making USDC the first stablecoin supported there. The bank already custodies USDC's reserves and plans to add more issuers, deepening regulated stablecoin infrastructure following the 2025 GENIUS Act.

title: Vitalik Buterin says crypto’s most powerful idea isn't nearly ready for use
blurb: Vitalik Buterin published the first part of a technical series on program obfuscation, calling indistinguishability obfuscation (iO) the most powerful idea in cryptography while stressing it is nowhere near practical. iO scrambles a program so it still runs and returns the same outputs while hiding its internal logic, which Buterin frames as a near-universal "trustless trusted third party" for uses like collusion-resistant voting. Recent work shows iO can be built under reasonable assumptions, but runtimes remain so slow it sits at a research milestone rather than production.

title: Vitalik Buterin calls obfuscation cryptography’s ‘final boss,’ but says current approaches remain wildly impractical
blurb: Vitalik Buterin published a blog post calling cryptographic obfuscation the "final boss of cryptography," focusing on indistinguishability obfuscation (iO) that lets a program run on cleartext inputs without revealing its code. Paired with a blockchain, iO could enable collusion-resistant voting and other protocols approaching a "trustless trusted third party." Buterin notes current constructions remain impractical, stacking primitives like FHE and lattice tools into runtimes he calls "literally galactic," with optimization, bolder assumptions, or non-lattice methods as paths forward.

title: Galaxy Research cuts Clarity Act 2026 passage odds to 50%, citing Senate calendar concerns
blurb: Galaxy Research cut its odds of the Clarity Act becoming law in 2026 to 50%, down from 60% earlier this month, citing a tightening Senate calendar and stalled negotiations rather than the bill's substance. With no floor date or motion to proceed scheduled, analyst Alex Thorn said the crypto market structure bill could slip to September and run into midterm dynamics unless Senate leadership announces floor time by early July.

title: Ripple wants institutions to borrow against tokenized assets on XRPL
blurb: Ripple proposed an XRPL Lending Protocol that lets institutions borrow against tokenized on-chain assets, with the ledger enforcing pooling, interest accrual, repayment, and defaults while credit underwriting stays with off-chain human teams. Defined in the XLS-65 and XLS-66 drafts and currently live only on a test network, the standard targets institutional short-term financing against assets like RLUSD reserves and still needs validator approval to activate.

title: Strategy opens door to selling billions of bitcoin under new capital plan. Here's what it means
blurb: Strategy authorized a new bitcoin monetization program under its Digital Credit Capital Framework, allowing BTC sales to fund a USD reserve, cover preferred dividends and interest, and finance up to $2B in buybacks. The filing sets no overall cap on sales but ties them to specific board-approved purposes, including $1.25B earmarked for the reserve, with MSTR shares up 3% on the news.

title: Changpeng Zhao says Binance's MiCA application in Greece was fully compliant and close to approval by at least one EU regulator but "other forces" opposed it (Naga Avan-Nomayo/The Block)
blurb: Changpeng Zhao said Binance's MiCA license application in Greece was fully compliant and near approval by at least one EU regulator before "other forces" intervened to block it. The comments highlight ongoing friction between Binance and European regulators under the MiCA framework, which the exchange needs to navigate to operate legally across the EU's 27 member states.

title: DraftKings Launches Proprietary Prediction Markets Exchange With $3.4B Annualized Consumer Volume
blurb: DraftKings launched DKeX, a proprietary prediction markets exchange reporting roughly $3.4B in annualized consumer volume and $11.3B in total trading volume for the week ended June 21, with the 2026 FIFA World Cup cited as a key demand driver. The CFTC-licensed platform, built on its acquired Railbird Technologies, gives DraftKings full ownership of its predictions stack and immediate distribution to sports bettors across 30 states as it challenges Polymarket and Kalshi.

title: Filing: Strategy paused its bitcoin acquisitions last week, instead topping up its USD reserve to $2.55B and announcing a $1B digital credit buyback program (James Hunt/The Block)
blurb: Strategy paused its bitcoin purchases last week, per a new filing, instead lifting its USD reserve to $2.55B and authorizing a $1B buyback of its digital credit instruments. The pivot marks a shift toward balance-sheet management over accumulation for the largest corporate bitcoin holder, redirecting capital to liquidity and debt reduction rather than adding to its position.

title: Crypto analytics firm Chainalysis proposes standards for blockchain tracing
blurb: Chainalysis published a proposed ontology for blockchain tracing, breaking address clusters into component parts so investigators can gauge how much confidence the underlying data supports. The firm built the standard on its experience in the DOJ's case against Bitcoin Fog co-founder Roman Sterlingov, where a Daubert hearing ruled its Reactor tool reliable enough for prosecution.

title: Private keys, not smart contracts, caused 40% of crypto's $16 billion hack losses. Here's whats being done.
blurb: Stolen private keys account for about 40% of the $16.69 billion lost to crypto hacks, exceeding losses from smart contract or blockchain flaws, according to DeFiLlama data cited by CoinDesk. Most breaches trace to key-management and operational failures across people, systems, and third-party tools rather than broken cryptography. Teams are adopting multi-party computation and account abstraction to remove the single-key dependency and make compromised credentials harder to exploit.

title: Ukraine transfers $8.3 million in seized crypto amid potential plans for strategic reserve
blurb: Ukraine transferred more than $8.3M in seized USDT to ARMA, its asset-recovery agency, marking the first time the state has taken custody of confiscated crypto, according to the Prosecutor General's Office. The move arrives as Kyiv weighs a strategic crypto reserve funded by criminally forfeited assets, echoing the US model, though the funds remain in custody rather than formally confiscated pending conviction.

title: CFTC Expands Polymarket Probe to Staged Trades and Fake Wins
blurb: The CFTC expanded its Polymarket investigation to cover staged trades and fabricated winning bets, extending a probe that began as an influencer advertising-disclosure inquiry into market-conduct territory, per Bloomberg. The scrutiny follows a bipartisan Senate letter citing a WSJ finding that Polymarket paid creators to film roughly $1.9M in fake wagers on dummy sites, with a July 10 response deadline set for the agency.

title: Bernstein says prediction-market infrastructure rush could spark wave of M&A across consumer platforms
blurb: Bernstein said the rush to build prediction-market infrastructure could trigger a wave of M&A across consumer trading and betting platforms, pointing to DraftKings' DKeX launch as a move to keep prediction-market revenue in-house. The bank expects further consolidation as exchanges, sportsbooks, and brokers compete to own the underlying matching and settlement layer rather than route flow to third parties.

title: Saylor's Strategy initiates buybacks and bitcoin monetization program, lifts STRC dividend
blurb: Strategy (MSTR) adopted a new Digital Credit Capital Framework, authorizing up to $1 billion in preferred-security buybacks and $1 billion in common-stock repurchases, raising the STRC preferred dividend to 12%, and approving a bitcoin monetization program that permits future BTC sales to fund reserves and dividends. The framework marks a shift from continuous capital issuance toward active balance-sheet management, with Strategy's $2.55 billion USD reserve now covering roughly 17.4 months of preferred obligations.

title: JPMorgan sees limited institutional demand for perpetual futures
blurb: JPMorgan said its client checks found little institutional demand for perpetual futures beyond speculative trading, citing basis risk, the absence of term structure and physical delivery, and clearing concerns as barriers to adoption. The bank argued perps offer few benefits over legacy derivatives for hedgers, and noted that offshore trading remains concentrated among a small number of large participants, raising questions about scalability.

title: Strategy’s MSTR, STRC shares recover after brutal week as Saylor unveils new buyback plans
blurb: Strategy's MSTR and STRC shares rebounded Monday after a brutal week that drove MSTR to a two-year low near $82 and a 30% slide from its June 22 high, as Saylor introduced a Digital Credit Capital Framework authorizing up to $1B in preferred-stock buybacks and $1B in MSTR repurchases. The company also paused bitcoin purchases, raised STRC's monthly dividend to 12%, and boosted its USD reserve to $2.55B.

title: New MiCA regime in Europe, U.S. jobs report for June: Crypto Week Ahead
blurb: The European Union's MiCA transitional period expires July 1, ending grandfathered operations for exchanges that haven't secured local licenses and prompting rivals to court Binance's regional users after the firm failed to obtain one. Traders also face a dense US macro week, with June nonfarm payrolls expected at 114K on July 2, a Robinhood product event, and Securitize's NYSE debut via SPAC.

title: Japanese prediction market startups Miraima and Poyp are utilizing a "point-to-voucher" system to bypass strict anti-gambling laws, in a bid to rival Polymarket (Bloomberg)
blurb: Japanese prediction market startups Miraima and Poyp are using a "point-to-voucher" model that converts winnings into retailer vouchers rather than cash, sidestepping Japan's strict anti-gambling laws as they target Polymarket's market. The structure lets them operate domestically without a gambling license, opening a regulated path into a sector where US-based Polymarket remains legally blocked from American users.

title: Binance founder CZ says MiCA application was ‘fully compliant’ and near approval before political forces intervened
blurb: Binance founder Changpeng Zhao said the exchange's Greek MiCA license application was fully compliant and near approval by at least one EU regulator before unspecified political forces intervened, prompting last week's withdrawal. Binance now plans to seek authorization in another member state ahead of the July 1 deadline, after which unlicensed firms must cease EU operations, with Spain's regulator confirming no extensions.

title: Pump.fun's PUMP Buybacks Top $400M as Token Stays Flat
blurb: Pump.fun has repurchased and burned over $400M of its PUMP token across 346 days, removing roughly 145.5 billion tokens from circulation under the 50%-of-revenue buyback policy it adopted in April. The burns have yet to lift the price, with PUMP down about 16% over the past 30 days and trading roughly 83% below its September record as memecoin fee generation cools to $23.5M monthly.

title: Kalshi and Polymarket could become M&A targets as prediction markets consolidate: Bernstein
blurb: Bernstein said Kalshi and Polymarket could become acquisition targets as prediction-market platforms vertically integrate, citing DraftKings' Railbird buy, Coinbase's acquisition of The Clearing Company, and Robinhood's Susquehanna-backed Rothera exchange. Both firms own the full exchange stack but trail on consumer distribution, the broker noted, leaving each as plausibly a target as an acquirer amid converging sportsbook, exchange, and consumer-finance competition.

title: Bitmine lifts Ethereum treasury to 5.7 million ETH through ‘challenging’ weekly slide, joins Russell 1000
blurb: Bitmine Immersion Technologies added 27,084 ETH last week to reach 5.70 million ETH, or 4.7% of supply, putting it 94% of the way to its target of holding 5% of Ethereum, even as ETH fell 8% on the week. The company was added to the Russell 1000 on June 26, a reconstitution Tom Lee expects to draw passive funds and ETFs into BMNR shares.

title: Europe’s unlicensed crypto firms face ‘wipeout’ as MiCA deadline hits
blurb: ESMA called on unauthorized crypto-asset service providers to wind down in an orderly manner as MiCA's transitional period ends July 1, when pre-MiCA national registrations expire across the EEA. Only 244 firms hold MiCA licenses against more than 3,000 registered VASPs in 2024, and OKX Europe's CEO estimates 80% of crypto players won't survive the combined MiCA, EMI, and payment-institution compliance burden.

title: BlackRock Adds Ethena's Synthetic Dollar to Its $20T Aladdin Risk Management Platform
blurb: BlackRock will list Ethena's USDe synthetic dollar as an approved digital asset on Aladdin, its risk platform managing over $20T for banks, insurers, and pension funds, while BUIDL becomes the primary reserve for Ethena's whitelabel stablecoins. The tie-up is the deepest link yet between the largest asset manager and a DeFi derivatives protocol, signaling traditional finance is moving capital onchain rather than building rival rails.

title: OKX and Coinbase Race to Sign Up Binance's Displaced EU Users Before MiCA Deadline
blurb: OKX and Coinbase launched competing deposit-bonus campaigns to capture European users losing access to Binance, which restricts EEA services on July 1 after withdrawing its MiCA license application in Greece. OKX, licensed in Malta, is offering up to 8% on transfers, while Coinbase is targeting Coinbase One subscribers across seven markets, both exploiting MiCA's licensing requirement as a structural moat against unauthorized rivals.

title: India’s USDT premium tops 8.5% as crypto remittance crackdown squeezes stablecoin supply: report
blurb: India's USDT premium climbed above 8.5%, more than double its usual 3-4% range, after Enforcement Directorate raids on Bengaluru crypto remittance firms disrupted the stablecoin supply pipeline, per The Economic Times. The crackdown targets a roughly two-year-old workaround in which NRIs used USDT to bypass FEMA documentation requirements, and lands days before Parliament's finance committee meets the RBI on July 2 to weigh virtual-asset rules.

title: BNY Adds USDC to Institutional Custody Platform in Expanded Circle Partnership
blurb: BNY, the world's largest custodian with $59.4T in assets under custody, made USDC the first stablecoin supported on its Digital Asset Custody platform, letting institutional clients hold, transfer, mint, and redeem Circle's token alongside traditional assets. The integration, enabled by the GENIUS Act's custody authority for chartered banks, collapses the handoff between traditional custodian and stablecoin-infra provider into one operational framework, with BNY planning to add more issuers over time.

title: MiCA's looming deadline could leave 10 million crypto users without a platform in the EU
blurb: MiCA takes full effect on July 1, forcing dozens of unlicensed exchanges to halt EU services and potentially displacing more than 10 million users toward MiCA-authorized providers or self-hosted wallets. Industry executives estimate up to 80% of Europe's roughly 3,000 pre-MiCA VASPs will not survive the deadline, with the EBA separately proposing fines of up to 12.5% of annual turnover for non-compliant stablecoin issuers.

title: BlackRock’s Aladdin platform adds deeper support for Ethena’s stablecoin products
blurb: BlackRock and Ethena Labs deepened their partnership, adding support for Ethena's synthetic dollar USDe on Aladdin, BlackRock's institutional portfolio and risk-management platform, alongside a $100M liquidity facility for BUIDL routed through Securitize. Eligible BUIDL holders can now swap tokens for USDC, USDtb, and other stablecoins, and convert back outside market hours, tightening interoperability between tokenized Treasury funds and onchain dollar rails.

title: Strategy Authorizes up to $1.25B of Bitcoin Sales as Saylor Formalizes Capital Pivot
blurb: Strategy's board approved a Digital Credit Capital Framework authorizing up to $1.25B in Bitcoin sales to fund dividends, interest, and buybacks, formalizing that the largest corporate BTC holder may now sell from its 847,363 BTC stack. The move codifies a capital pivot for a company built on never selling, arriving as its STRC preferred stock trades below target and its Bitcoin position sits roughly $14B underwater.

title: BlackRock pushes deeper into DeFi with Ethena integration, sending ENA up 8%
blurb: BlackRock will integrate Ethena's yield-generating USDe token into its Aladdin risk platform, which oversees risk for more than $20T in assets, and stand up a $100M liquidity facility for its tokenized money market fund BUIDL. The facility lets eligible BUIDL holders swap into USDC, USDtb, and other stablecoins outside market hours, deepening the link between tokenized Treasuries and DeFi as ENA rose 8%.

title: BNY and Circle expand partnership, adding mint and burn capabilities for USDC
blurb: BNY expanded its partnership with Circle to let institutional clients mint and burn USDC directly through the bank, making it the first asset on BNY's Digital Asset Custody platform. Clients can now hold USDC in BNY custody wallets and instruct the world's largest custodian bank to convert dollars into newly minted USDC or redeem it back into fiat, with support for additional stablecoin issuers planned.

title: J.P. Morgan broadens blockchain settlement network as banks modernize cross-border payments
blurb: J.P. Morgan added five Asia-Pacific currencies to its Kinexys blockchain settlement platform, including the yen, renminbi, and Singapore dollar, bringing total support to eight currencies for round-the-clock cross-border settlement and FX. The expansion deepens the bank's reach into Asia for a network that has processed over $4 trillion to date, with Payoneer and JERA Global Markets among the first clients on the new rails.

title: JPMorgan backs U.S. crypto bill but warns of risks in digital asset framework
blurb: JPMorgan endorsed the Digital Asset Market Clarity Act but spent much of its blog post warning that crypto assets, exchanges and brokers behaving like securities firms should face the same disclosure, investor-protection and market-integrity rules as traditional finance. The bank pushed lawmakers to impose bank-like capital, liquidity and AML requirements on stablecoins and tokenized deposits, as the Senate races to resolve DeFi liability and stablecoin yield provisions before the August recess.

title: BIS warns stablecoins are more like ETFs than actual money, and they're creating FX risk
blurb: The Bank for International Settlements argued in its annual report that stablecoins behave more like ETF shares than money, citing secondary prices that drift from par and redemptions that aren't guaranteed to settle instantly at face value. The BIS also warned that dollar-pegged stablecoins are accelerating dollarization in vulnerable economies, undermining local currencies, evading capital controls, and creating FX risk without ever settling on central bank balance sheets.

title: White House to speak with law enforcement groups to push Crypto's Clarity Act
blurb: The White House plans to meet Monday with law enforcement groups objecting to the Senate's Digital Asset Market Clarity Act, aiming to resolve disputes over Section 604, which shields DeFi software developers from being classified as money transmitters. Groups like the National Sheriffs Association argue the provision hands mixers and tumblers a blanket exemption, while the industry counters that the bill adds crime-fighting tools and its failure would leave enforcement in limbo.

title: Tether's USDT jumps to 8.5% premium in India after crypto payment crackdown
blurb: USDT's price on Indian crypto platforms surged to more than 8.5% above its dollar peg, roughly double the usual 3% to 4% spread, after India's Enforcement Directorate raided five crypto payment firms in Bengaluru and choked off the token's supply. The ED alleges the firms moved over $265 million in unauthorized cross-border transfers using USDT, and market makers have since pulled back from sourcing the stablecoin abroad.

title: Wall Street's BNY expands stablecoin services for institutions, starting with Circle's USDC
blurb: BNY, the world's largest custody bank with $59T in assets under custody, will let institutional clients hold, mint, and redeem Circle's USDC through its Digital Asset Custody platform, the first stablecoin supported there. The bank already custodies USDC's reserves, so the addition lets institutions manage cash and stablecoins on one platform, deepening regulated stablecoin infrastructure following the 2025 GENIUS Act.

title: Strategy pauses bitcoin buys, establishes $1B digital credit repurchase program as USD reserve tops $2.5B
blurb: Strategy paused its bitcoin purchases between June 22 and 28 despite raising $1.15B from MSTR share sales, leaving holdings flat at 847,363 BTC while it grew its USD reserve to $2.55B and authorized a $1B buyback of its STRC, STRF, STRD, and STRK digital credit securities. A new board-approved policy now requires the reserve to cover at least 12 months of preferred dividends and interest, a shift toward funding obligations rather than accumulating bitcoin.

title: Strategy opens door to selling billions of bitcoin under new capital plan. Here's what it means
blurb: Strategy authorized a new bitcoin monetization program under its Digital Credit Capital Framework, its first formal approval to sell BTC to fund a USD reserve, cover preferred dividends and interest, and finance up to $2B in buybacks. The filing sets no overall cap on sales but ties them to board-approved purposes, marking a shift for the largest corporate bitcoin holder from pure accumulation toward balance-sheet management.

title: Ripple wants institutions to borrow against tokenized assets on XRPL
blurb: Ripple proposed an XRPL Lending Protocol that lets institutions borrow against tokenized on-chain assets, with the ledger enforcing pooling, interest, repayment, and defaults while credit underwriting stays off-chain with human teams. The standard, defined in drafts XLS-65 and XLS-66, is live only on a test network and still needs validator approval, positioning Ripple against Aave and Compound with fixed network-level rules.

title: Galaxy Research cuts Clarity Act 2026 passage odds to 50%, citing Senate calendar concerns
blurb: Galaxy Research lowered its odds of the Clarity Act becoming law in 2026 to 50%, down from 60% earlier this month, citing a tightening Senate calendar and stalled negotiations rather than the crypto market-structure bill's substance. With no floor date set and President Trump's SAVE Act demand squeezing the schedule, Galaxy's Alex Thorn said passage could slip to September and run into midterm dynamics.

title: Changpeng Zhao says Binance's MiCA application in Greece was fully compliant and close to approval by at least one EU regulator but "other forces" opposed it (Naga Avan-Nomayo/The Block)
blurb: Changpeng Zhao claimed Binance's MiCA license application in Greece was fully compliant and near approval by at least one EU regulator before "other forces" intervened to block it. The remark spotlights how Binance's path to a unified EU passport under MiCA remains contested, even as rivals like Coinbase and Kraken secure authorization to operate across the bloc.

title: DraftKings Launches Proprietary Prediction Markets Exchange With $3.4B Annualized Consumer Volume
blurb: DraftKings launched DKeX, a proprietary prediction markets exchange built on the CFTC license it acquired through Railbird, moving off third-party infrastructure to own the full stack behind its event-contracts business. The platform reported roughly $3.4B in annualized consumer volume and $11.3B in total trading volume for the week ended June 21, and its 30-state sportsbook footprint gives it immediate distribution against Polymarket and Kalshi.

title: Filing: Strategy paused its bitcoin acquisitions last week, instead topping up its USD reserve to $2.55B and announcing a $1B digital credit buyback program (James Hunt/The Block)
blurb: Strategy halted its bitcoin purchases last week, according to a new filing, instead growing its USD reserve to $2.55B and launching a $1B buyback of its digital credit securities. The pause marks a shift from the company's accumulate-at-all-costs treasury strategy toward shoring up liquidity and supporting its own credit instruments, a notable signal from the largest corporate bitcoin holder.

title: Cook Stays at Fed But Trump Wins Power Over Other Agencies
blurb: The Supreme Court ruled that Federal Reserve governor Lisa Cook cannot be removed without proof of wrongdoing, reaffirming the central bank's insulation from presidential pressure even as the same decision expanded Trump's authority to fire officials at other independent agencies. The carve-out preserves market confidence in Fed rate-setting independence while leaving regulators like the CFPB and FDIC more exposed to White House control over banking-policy direction.

title: Ukraine transfers $8.3 million in seized crypto amid potential plans for strategic reserve
blurb: Ukraine transferred over $8.3M in seized USDT to ARMA, its asset recovery agency, marking the first time the country has moved confiscated crypto into state management, according to the Prosecutor General's Office. The transfer lands as Ukraine weighs a strategic crypto reserve funded by forfeited assets, mirroring the US approach, though ARMA holds custody of the USDT rather than ownership, which requires a conviction.

title: CFTC Expands Polymarket Probe to Staged Trades and Fake Wins
blurb: The CFTC expanded its Polymarket investigation to cover staged trades and fabricated winning bets, Bloomberg reported, widening a probe that began as an advertising-disclosure inquiry into the platform's paid-influencer scheme. The escalation follows a bipartisan Senate letter citing a WSJ finding that paid creators filmed roughly $900,000 in fake wins on dummy sites, and it revives jurisdictional questions about whether the CFTC can police event-contract platforms as a gaming regulator.

title: Trump threatens tariffs over EU digital taxes
blurb: Trump threatened 100% tariffs on any country that imposes a digital services tax on American companies, escalating a long-running dispute with the EU over levies that hit firms like Stripe, Visa, and PayPal alongside Big Tech. The threat raises the cost of EU expansion for US payments and fintech players, who would face both the local DSTs and the downstream trade fallout if Washington retaliates.

title: Saylor's Strategy initiates buybacks and bitcoin monetization program, lifts STRC dividend
blurb: Strategy adopted a Digital Credit Capital Framework that authorizes up to $1B in preferred-security buybacks and $1B in common-stock repurchases, raises the STRC preferred dividend to 12% effective July 1, and creates a bitcoin monetization program permitting future BTC sales. The framework marks a shift from capital issuance toward active balance-sheet management, with a $2.55B USD reserve covering roughly 17 months of preferred dividend and interest obligations.

title: AirTrunk Is Said to Be Near Confidential Filing for Biggest Singapore IPO in Years
blurb: AirTrunk, the Blackstone-backed data center operator, is near a confidential filing for a Singapore REIT IPO that would rank as the city-state's largest in years. The listing signals continued investor appetite for data center infrastructure as a yield-bearing asset class, channeling AI-driven compute demand into public-market real estate vehicles.

title: Scaling Network Analysis for Fraud Prevention with BigQuery Graph
blurb: Curve, the UK financial super-app, moved its fraud-detection network analysis to BigQuery Graph, using native GQL to trace the multi-hop links between accounts that share devices, funding cards, or contact details. Running graph queries directly inside its existing BigQuery warehouse let Curve surface organized fraud rings without migrating to a dedicated graph database, avoiding costly self-joins and producing significant transaction savings.

title: Bernstein says prediction-market infrastructure rush could spark wave of M&A across consumer platforms
blurb: Bernstein said the rush to build prediction-market infrastructure could trigger a wave of M&A across consumer trading and betting platforms, pointing to DraftKings' DKeX launch as a move to keep prediction-market revenue in-house. The analysts argue that as exchanges, sportsbooks, and brokerages race to own the rails, vertical integration and acquisitions will reshape who controls order flow in the fast-growing category.

title: JPMorgan sees limited institutional demand for perpetual futures
blurb: JPMorgan said its client due diligence found little institutional demand for perpetual futures, concluding that most activity is speculative leveraged trading rather than genuine hedging by producers or consumers with underlying exposure. The bank flagged basis risk, the absence of term structure and physical delivery, missing clearing protections, and concentration among a few large offshore players as the barriers keeping perps from displacing traditional derivatives.

title: New MiCA regime in Europe, U.S. jobs report for June: Crypto Week Ahead
blurb: The EU's MiCA transitional period expires July 1, ending the grandfathering window that let crypto firms operate without full authorization, with rivals now courting Binance users in the region after it failed to secure a license. The same week brings June nonfarm payrolls (est. 114K, down from 172K), Eurozone inflation, ISM manufacturing, and a Robinhood product reveal, setting up a macro-heavy stretch for digital-asset markets.

title: Japanese prediction market startups Miraima and Poyp are utilizing a "point-to-voucher" system to bypass strict anti-gambling laws, in a bid to rival Polymarket (Bloomberg)
blurb: Japanese prediction-market startups Miraima and Poyp are routing user activity through a "point-to-voucher" system to sidestep the country's strict anti-gambling laws as they build domestic rivals to Polymarket. The workaround lets users convert winnings into vouchers rather than cash, threading a regulatory needle that has kept global prediction markets out of Japan and signaling demand the incumbents cannot yet legally serve.

title: Czech cybersecurity startup Wultra lands €6.8 million to support Europe’s digital identity wallet rollout
blurb: Wultra, a Prague-based provider of post-quantum authentication and digital identity software for banks and fintechs, raised €6.8M in a Series A led by Seventure Partners. The startup will scale its quantum-resistant, passwordless authentication platform to support the European Digital Identity Wallet rollout, building on a base of 70-plus financial clients across 25 countries as institutions move to phishing-resistant, quantum-ready infrastructure.

title: UK founders signed the most investor-friendly terms in 5 years in 2025 and most of them didn’t know it
blurb: UK startups raised £11.2B across 711 term sheets in 2025, up from £7.6B in 2024, but HSBC Innovation Banking found the underlying terms shifted toward investor control at the fastest rate in five years. Founders largely missed the change, signing liquidation preferences, board provisions, and anti-dilution clauses that tighten downside protection for VCs even as headline funding totals climbed.

title: Verizon Sees Quarterly Loss of Up to $800 Million Due to BT Deal
blurb: Verizon projected a second-quarter loss of $700M to $800M tied to a joint venture with BT Group that combines the two carriers' international businesses. The charge reflects the upfront cost of consolidating overseas operations into a shared entity, a structure that lets both telcos offload subscale international units while retaining exposure to any future upside.

title: Binance founder CZ says MiCA application was ‘fully compliant’ and near approval before political forces intervened
blurb: Binance founder Changpeng Zhao said the exchange's Greek MiCA license application was fully compliant and near approval from at least one EU regulator before unspecified political forces intervened, prompting last week's withdrawal. Binance now plans to seek authorization in another member state ahead of the July 1 deadline, after which unlicensed firms must cease EU operations, with Spain's regulator ruling out any extensions.

title: Kalshi and Polymarket could become M&A targets as prediction markets consolidate: Bernstein
blurb: Bernstein said Kalshi and Polymarket could become acquisition targets as prediction-market platforms vertically integrate, blurring the lines between exchanges, brokerages, and sportsbooks. The broker pointed to recent moves like DraftKings buying Railbird, Coinbase acquiring The Clearing Company, and Robinhood partnering with Susquehanna, arguing that firms owning both consumer distribution and exchange infrastructure are capturing more economics in-house and pressuring rivals to consolidate.

title: Quantifind, whose AI products help banks combat financial crimes such as money laundering, raised $200M led by Summit Partners (Laura Kreutzer/Wall Street Journal)
blurb: Quantifind, which builds AI tools that help banks detect money laundering and other financial crimes, raised $200M led by Summit Partners. The funding lands as regulators sharpen AML enforcement and banks face pressure to cut false-positive rates in transaction monitoring, pushing institutions toward machine-learning screening over legacy rules-based systems.
